steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to describe the study's research methodology — how the study was set up and conducted, not its goals or its results. A methodology description covers the experimental design: what was tested and how groups were structured.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The team of researchers tested the germination of 125 *Piper sancti-felicis* seeds that had been ingested by chestnut short-tailed bats and 125 *Piper sancti-felicis* seeds that had not been ingested.": Describes the experimental design — two groups of 125 seeds tested under different conditions. This is the methodology. ✓
  • "J.E. López and C. Vaughan wanted to explore the effects of chestnut short-tailed bats' ingestion on *Piper sancti-felicis* seed germination.": States the researchers' goal, not how they carried out the study. ✗
  • "In the study, a lower percentage of *Piper sancti-felicis* seeds ingested by bats germinated than those that had not been ingested by bats.": Reports a finding from the results, not the method used. ✗
  • "Research by J.E. López and C. Vaughan revealed that 82 of 125--that is, 66%--of the *Piper sancti-felicis* seeds that had been ingested by the bats germinated.": States a specific result (66% germination rate), not the experimental structure. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
